title: phegopteris × thwaitesii
intro: Phegopteris × thwaitesii, also known as Thwaites' maidenhair spleenwort, is a hybrid fern species in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming the plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and aiding in the management of diabetes. Thwaites' maidenhair spleenwort can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture for medicinal purposes.
